subsection.heading: Complaint
subsection.content: A criminal or civil complaint shall be filed no later than 24 hours after a case number is assigned by the clerk.

subsection.heading: Appearance
subsection.content: A defendant shall appear upon a summons no later than 72 hours after service thereof, and in a civil case, shall file a response to the complaint no later than 72 hours after their first appearance.

subsection.heading: Pretrial motions
subsection.content: All pretrial motions shall be filed no later than 96 hours after the defendant’s first appearance, or in civil cases, after the defendant’s response to the complaint, unless extended for good cause by the court.

subsection.heading: Response to motions
subsection.content: A response to a motion shall be filed no later than 48 hours after the filing of such motion.

subsection.heading: Judicial discretion
subsection.content: The court may shorten or extend any deadline in this section for good cause, on motion or sua sponte.

subsection.heading: Matters under advisement
subsection.content: The court shall rule on any submitted pretrial motion, or enter an advisement/status order with a decision deadline, within 72 hours, and no matter shall remain under advisement more than seven (7) days absent written findings of good cause.

note.heading: USAR Amendments
note.p: Pub. L. 38–266, § 5, repealed and replaced former section 3163 as part of the replacement of chapter 208.
